title: Residual Blocks
concept: A residual block asks stacked layers to learn the useful adjustment to the input, then adds that adjustment back to the original signal instead of rebuilding the whole representation from scratch.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the basic job of a skip connection?

options.text: To let information travel around some layers through a direct path.
To reset all gradients to zero after each batch.
To replace the loss function with a different optimizer.

exercises.explain: Exactly. That direct path sets up the main idea behind residual blocks.

cards.title: Renovate the room, don't rebuild the house
cards.body: If a room is already mostly good, the smart move is usually to repaint a wall, change the lights, or add shelves — not demolish the whole house and start over. Residual blocks use that same philosophy: learn the needed adjustment, not a complete replacement.

cards.title: Learning the residual
cards.body: A residual block combines a normal learned path with a skip path. Instead of asking the stacked layers to produce the entire desired output from scratch, it helps them learn the residual — the change needed to improve the input. The original signal is then added back in.

cards.title: Editing a draft instead of rewriting it
cards.body: Suppose a student already has a decent essay draft. A teacher's notes might say: tighten the intro, clarify one paragraph, fix two awkward sentences. Those notes are smaller changes layered on top of something useful. Residual learning works like those notes.

cards.title: Sharpen the useful features
cards.body: Imagine a CNN block receiving feature maps that already capture the rough outline of a cat. The next useful step may be to sharpen whiskers or strengthen ear edges, not to rediscover 'cat outline' from zero. A residual block encourages that kind of targeted correction.

cards.title: Residual does not mean tiny forever
cards.body: A common myth is that residual blocks can only learn tiny cosmetic tweaks. In reality, if the task needs a big change, the learned residual can be large. The point is not to force smallness. The point is to make 'copy the useful part, change what needs changing' easy.

cards.title: Whole rewrite vs. useful adjustment
cards.body: Two ways a block can think about its job:

left.text:
Rebuild the whole output
from the block's input
every time

right.text:
Keep the input path alive
Learn the change that helps
then add it back

exercises.prompt: What is the main idea of a residual block?

options.text: Let the learned layers focus on the change to the input, then combine that with the original signal.
Force every block to erase the input before computing its output.
Train only one neuron per layer and freeze the rest.
Replace activation functions with random noise.

exercises.explain: Correct. Residual blocks keep the original pathway available and learn the adjustment that improves it.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Residual blocks try to throw away the original input and replace it completely every time.
exercises.explain: False. The original input is preserved through the skip path, while the learned branch adds an improvement.

exercises.sentence: Residual learning focuses on the ___ between the input and the improved output.
exercises.explain: Exactly. The residual is the change or correction the block needs to contribute.

exercises.prompt: Which situation best fits residual learning?

options.text: A block receives mostly useful features and mainly needs to refine them.
A network deletes its input before every forward pass.
The optimizer stops updating any layer that already works a little.

exercises.explain: Right. Residual blocks shine when preserving good existing information is easier than rebuilding everything from scratch.

exercises.prompt: A feature map already captures the rough shape of an object, but it still needs clearer edges and cleaner texture cues. Why is a residual block a natural fit here?

options.text: Because it can keep the useful base features and learn the extra correction needed on top.
Because it permanently disables the original feature map so a new one must replace it.
Because residual blocks only work when no useful features exist yet.
Because the skip path automatically solves the task without any learned layers.

exercises.explain: Exactly. Residual blocks are great when the network should preserve a lot of what it already knows and add a targeted improvement.
